template.name: Task
template.declaration: clock x; /* execution progress clock */ clock period_clk; /* period tracking */ int[0,20] rem; /* remaining compute units */ int[0,20] cs_rem; /* remaining CS units */ bool has_lock; /* currently holds S_R */

transition.label: period_clk >= T_period
period_clk := 0, rem := C, cs_rem := cs_len, has_lock := false, act_prio[id] := nom, block_dur[id] := 0, x := 0
period_clk == 0
rem := C, cs_rem := cs_len, has_lock := false, act_prio[id] := nom, block_dur[id] := 0, x := 0
act_prio[id] <= act_prio[0] && act_prio[id] <= act_prio[1] && act_prio[id] <= act_prio[2] && rem > 0 && uses_R == 0
x := 0
act_prio[id] <= act_prio[0] && act_prio[id] <= act_prio[1] && act_prio[id] <= act_prio[2] && rem > 0 && uses_R == 1 && !has_lock
lock_req[id]!
lock_ack[id]?
has_lock := true, x := 0, blocking_tau1 := (id == 0) ? false : blocking_tau1
lock_ack[id]?
has_lock := true, x := 0, blocking_tau1 := (id == 0) ? false : blocking_tau1
x >= 1 && cs_rem == 1 && rem == 1
unlock_rel[id]!
has_lock := false, cs_rem := 0, rem := 0, act_prio[id] := nom

(id == 0 && (act_prio[1] < act_prio[0] || act_prio[2] < act_prio[0])) || (id == 1 && (act_prio[0] < act_prio[1] || act_prio[2] < act_prio[1])) || (id == 2 && (act_prio[0] < act_prio[2] || act_prio[1] < act_prio[2]))

template.name: ResourceManager

transition.label: holder == 2
lock_req[0]?
wait_mask := wait_mask | 1, blocking_tau1 := true, block_dur[0] := 0, /* PIP Eq(7.8): holder inherits max(prio_holder, prio_blocked) */ act_prio[2] := (P1 < act_prio[2]) ? P1 : act_prio[2]
holder == 0 && (wait_mask & 4) != 0
unlock_rel[0]?
holder := 2, pending_id := 2, wait_mask := wait_mask & ~4, act_prio[0] := P1, /* restore tau1 */ /* tau3's blocking time recorded */ block_dur[2] := 0
holder == 2 && (wait_mask & 1) != 0
unlock_rel[2]?
holder := 0, pending_id := 0, wait_mask := wait_mask & ~1, /* PIP restore: tau3 returns to nominal prio (Buttazzo §7.6.4 p.225) */ act_prio[2] := P3, blocking_tau1 := false

query.comment: Property 1: No deadlock. Verifies that the system never reaches a state where no transition is enabled and time cannot progress. Buttazzo §7.6.5 notes PIP does NOT prevent deadlock in general (Fig.7.13), but our 3-task / 1-resource scenario with total semaphore ordering cannot deadlock: tau1 and tau3 use one resource in the same order.

query.formula: A[] (blocking_tau1 imply not (tau2.Running and not blocked_tau2))
query.comment: Property 2: PIP prevents unbounded priority inversion. When tau1 (highest priority) is blocked on S_R held by tau3, tau2 (medium priority, no resource overlap) must NOT run freely. This is the exact correctness condition PIP enforces (Buttazzo §7.6, p.214): push-through blocking (§7.6.1, p.216) ensures tau2 cannot preempt tau3-boosted-to-P1.

Property 3: Blocking time bounds (Buttazzo §7.6.3, Eq 7.9-7.11). B1=4 (tau3 CS length), B2=4 (push-through, Lemma 7.1), B3=0. These are worst-case bounds; the verified system should never exceed them.

query.formula: A[] (tau1.Idle or tau1.Ready or tau1.Running or tau1.CriticalSection or tau1.WaitAck or tau1.Blocked)
query.comment: Property 4: Liveness / state coverage. tau1 is always in a valid state (system never loses track of a task). Combined with Property 1, ensures tau1 eventually completes.
